preamble: Deaffrication. The voiced alveolar affricate [dz] simplifies to a simple voiced alveolar stop [d], losing its fricative release. This process occurred during various historical stages in Slavic and Romance languages.

examples.note: Historical deaffrication in various Slavic branches.
Occurred during the evolution from Vulgar Latin.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
